mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return rank IC on RSAFS month-over-month growth is below 0.05 over any two-year window, or the predictive correlation fails to be positive and statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below a threshold signals a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE drops below 5.5%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the success ratio (positive returns) is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate synchronized economic strength, amplifying positive earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 returns upward over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint month-over-month growth of INDPRO and PAYEMS does not produce a positive rank IC above 0.03 when combined via a simple average z-score, measured over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate during a tightening cycle raises the discount rate on future earnings, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the unconditional rank IC of FEDFUNDS level on 60-day forward returns is greater than -0.02, or if during identified tightening periods (FEDFUNDS increases over 3 consecutive months) the negative return prediction fails in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of economic activity; an increase signals future construction, durable goods demand, and employment growth, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the predictive rank IC of HOUST month-over-month growth on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI (CPIAUCSL) rises above a threshold level (e.g., 270), it signals overheating inflationary pressures that force future monetary tightening, depressing equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after CPIAUCSL crosses above 270, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the success ratio (negative returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) reflects strong consumer fundamentals; the interaction amplifies earnings growth expectations, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If a combined signal (e.g., z-score of RSAFS growth minus z-score of UNRATE change) does not achieve a positive rank IC above 0.05 on 5-day forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime characterized by elevated unemployment, a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals improving labor conditions that drive consumer spending and equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods where UNRATE > 6% and declining, the average 20-day forward return is not positive or the predictive accuracy (direction match) is below 55% over all such occurrences in the test sample.
